div.head: The Ambassador in Cuba (Guggenheim) to the Acting Secretary of State
div.p: 120. My telephone conversation with Walter C. Thurston.Chief, Division of Latin American Affairs, Department of State. After an informal conversation with the Cuban Secretary of State, he informed me that he would instruct the Cuban Chargé in the United States to withdraw the note regarding an arms embargoA notation in ink reads, “Note not yet sent by Cuban Chargé. H. F. M.” because the revolutionary situation had changed. The Cuban Secretary of State understands that shipments of arms from the United States are being prohibited as effectively as they would be under a formal proclamation of an arms embargo.
